title: Director Indemnification Agreement
description: Protects startup directors and officers from personal liability, guaranteeing expense advancement and indemnity.
whatsCovered: Indemnification for legal fees, settlements, and judgments
Right to advancement of legal defense expenses
Fiduciary duty liability boundaries and D&O insurance
Governing law and arbitration of disputes
whenToUse: Use this agreement when appointing new board members or C-level officers to protect their personal assets from company litigation.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: DIRECTOR INDEMNIFICATION AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Director Indemnification Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Officer Defense Advancement
clauses.body: Company shall advance all legal expenses of {indemnitee_name} and maintain D&O liability insurance of at least {d_o_insurance_limit}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is expense advancement?
faq.answer: It requires the company to pay the director's legal defense bills as they arrive, rather than waiting for the final trial verdict.

faq.question: Does this protect against fraud?
faq.answer: No, indemnification agreements explicitly exclude coverage for willful misconduct, criminal acts, or deliberate fraud.
